column11:
1) Excessive long hours(minimum 12 hours,in case long distance it becomes 12-16 hours.
2) Holiday policies: no weekly offs, you are supposed to report on site even on public holidays, religious festivals and national holidays.you will also not get pl sl or cl as per offer letter easily.your boss will ask you to report on site by taking medicine for seek
3) Assignment in irrelevant departments: as a trainee, company can randomly assign you in any department without knowing your skills and you can not oppose for this.no rotation training is available.
4) Toxic work culture: you will get scolded by your boss even in notice period.they will behave like inhumane with you.totally abusive culture.i won't think they even deserve to be my boss
5) lack of basic facilitis : on the work area no amenities like drinking water, rest room, toilets and urinals are not available. On accommodation place no amenities are available & you can not complaint if you will then they will play with your ratings 
6) restrictions over higher education: company's policies disallowed trainees to pursue any kind of part time full time course.there is no guarantee to get company's sponsored education program 
7)less salary: for det salary is 20k in which he have to pay for mess charges upto 5k.living in metro cities with such a less salary is unjustified.
8)so many other reasons

They ask you to put in more hours but pay you less for it. As a contract employee, I have to complete all the paperwork by myself in order to get paid. There is no lunch provided, and no cab service, but they tell you to handle all the expenses yourself. They don't even act like people; they just show off one employee who purposefully left me out of my project and says things like "It's our decision, we can in or out anyone it we want to do." My sincere suggestion to any freshmen not to join this company is that everything is done on his own initiative. I regret wasting three years in this organization since there is no assistance for me from this guy.

column11:
1) Only punch in time. No punch-out time. So definitely you have to work extra hours. Even though you complete your work leaving on time will be a dream. 
2) 6 working days per week, 2nd and 4th Saturday holiday. Expected to work on even during festivals/holidays to support projects. 
3) Culture feels like working in an old government office, surrounded by drawing sheets and papers. 
4) No coffee machine/pantry. They have fixed timings at 11 am and 4 pm when coffee/tea will be provided. In the pantry, there will be seating options.
5) Managers have no empathy towards the team members and treat the team so badly and verbal abuse. same is taught to the team leaders and so the full team becomes a nightmare to work with.
6) 30minutes lunch time seems so less and lunch timings & snacks timings have to be strictly followed. If you are late then food will not be available. 
7) Feels like you are always on a run, you will be exhausted. 
8) I would not recommend this place for younger generations with an international work culture. Because you cannot tolerate when people scolding/screaming at you and constant comparison of you with your team members.

column11: The policies at L&T regarding leaves and travel are problematic. Not only are only the 2nd and 4th Saturdays off, but employees are also required to travel during their personal time for official purposes, even on Sundays or after office hours. Additionally, the restriction on using privileged leaves only three times a year, and in blocks of three, is excessive. It's unclear why such stringent rules are in place when employees are fulfilling their duties effectively. In contrast to modern practices adopted by many foreign companies, L&T's adherence to traditional policies may lead to a rising attrition rate over time.

column10: 1. Diverse Career Opportunities: L&T is a diverse conglomerate with interests in various sectors, including engineering, construction, technology, and more. This diversity can provide employees with a wide range of career opportunities and the chance to explore different industries. 2. **Innovation and Technology:** L&T is known for its focus on innovation and technology. Working here can give you the opportunity to be part of cutting-edge projects and contribute to technological advancements. 3. **Learning and Development:** L&T places a strong emphasis on employee development. You can benefit from training programs, mentorship, and continuous learning to enhance your skills and advance your career. 4. **Global Presence:** L&T has a global footprint, which can open up international career opportunities. If you have aspirations to work on an international stage, L&T can provide those options. 5. **Stability and Reputation:** L&T has a long and stable history, which has earned it a solid reputation in the industry. Working for a reputable company can provide job security and enhance your professional standing. 6. **Team Collaboration:** L&T fosters a collaborative work environment, which can be enriching and satisfying for employees. Teamwork and a supportive work culture can enhance your job experience. 7. **Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR):** L&T is committed to CSR activities and sustainability. Being part of a company with a strong social and environmental responsibility agenda can be fulfilling for those who are socially conscious. 8. **Leadership Opportunities:** L&T often promotes from within, offering employees the chance to advance into leadership roles. If you're ambitious and want to take on more significant responsibilities, this can be a positive aspect of working at L&T. 9. **Financial Rewards:** Competitive salaries and benefits are typically offered by L&T, making it an attractive choice for those seeking financial stability and rewards. 10. **Prestigious Projects:** L&T is involved in prestigious and large-scale projects, such as infrastructure development and engineering feats. Contributing to such projects can be professionally rewarding. 11. **Work-Life Balance:** L&T places importance on work-life balance and offers various programs and policies to support employee well-being. 12. **Strong Company Culture:** L&T's values and culture emphasize ethics, integrity, and sustainability. For individuals who align with these values, it can be a motivating and positive work environment.
column11: 1. **Bureaucracy:** L&T is a large and established organization, and with that often comes some level of bureaucracy. Decision-making processes may be more formal and slower compared to smaller, agile companies. 2. **Workload:** Depending on your role and the specific project you're assigned to, you might encounter periods of heavy workload and tight deadlines, which can be stressful. 3. **Limited Work-Life Balance in Some Roles:** While L&T emphasizes work-life balance, certain roles or projects may require overtime and weekend work to meet project deadlines. 4. **Geographic Mobility:** If you're looking for a job that allows you to stay in one location, L&T's project-based nature may require you to move or travel frequently. 5. **Hierarchical Structure:** Like many large organizations, L&T has a hierarchical structure that can lead to challenges in terms of quick communication and feedback, especially for those lower in the hierarchy. 6. **Competitive Work Environment:** The competitive nature of the industry and the company's size means that you may experience a higher level of competition among colleagues, which can be stressful for some individuals.

column10: The organization is a good place if you are planning to stay for a very long period of time (10 years or more). It allows you to do anything you wish for in your work field, you can be at the entry-level or manage the entire scenario. Compensation is good when you have been retained for more than 6 years. Performance-linked rewards are also good for all levels. As this is system driven organization, the majority of your work-life depends on your rapport with your in-charges and colleagues. If you are new to the industry, this organization will teach you a lot about the industry thru its conventional methods, but it gives you the choice to decide the path where you want to make your career.

column10:
1. Good for your resume, as L&T is a MNC company. 
2. Will receive salary on time. 
3. If you are lucky you will get bus or cab facility. 
4 . Mostly supervising jobs, which doest require to think more of engineering. 
5. Might receive subsidised lunch and dinner, based on project. 
6. Might receive accomodation with subsidised rate near to project spaces. 
7. Can get resignation within one month notice period.
column11:
1. What we do is a supervisor jobs not engineering. 
2. Even a polytechnic guy can do your job and mostly you will find polytechnic worker in your job role just dominating with stupid politics. 
3. Lot of regional politics - either you are a tamilian or a Gujarati, you will be   safe in the job. 
4. No designer roles or R&D roles.. mostly would be site roles mostly into supervising the projects. 
5. Exit opportunities would be very less, unless you want to go into same domain. Let say you are working in shipbuilding and if you want to move out to automobile manufacturing, it would be really difficult.
6.a big no to job rotations, let's say you wanna move into supply chain or designing roles, it's not that easy to shuffle. 
7. Lot of HR operations are mannual and you do not know the policies on how to get relieved from the current project and move to a domain that you are interested in.

column10:
Good for start of career, person will learn a lot due to practical approach.
1. HR provides lots of training opportunities
2. Very good day to day medical (OPD) coverage if you are in covenanted (Manager) cadre, generally it takes 5/6/7 year to reach there for a GET.
3. Professionally managed to some extent, not a lala company.
4. For construction professionals this company is better than other indian companies even if it has lot draw backs.
5. Salary structure is improving but it has taken nearly 2 decades to change.
column11:
1. Very old rating system - bell curve (force fitting of people in ratings even if person is brilliant)
2. Culture varies a lot with business units, for example - L&T construction is a worst place to work, no planning nothing, daily un wanted emergency.
3. Rating benchmarking is only on papers, it actually depends on many thing like business unit, boss, bell curve.
4. Salary is heavily dependent on ratings (for ex. - very high salaries for top 2 ratings on the scale, in comparisons to other people)
5. Very low transparency, communication from top management is also poor.
6. Do not spent too many years here unless you are not getting better options.

column3: My Experience as Graduate Engineer Trainee (GET) in L&T-ECC [Civil Engineering ]
column10:
1. Great Experienced Peers and obviously the best company in construction industry which uses advanced technology available. 
2. Rigorous Training of 11 months which improves your interpersonal and other soft skills
3. Freedom to take decision. risk and responsibility. This is the best thing about L&T you will get huge responsibility which improves your managerial skills drastically.
4. Working in L&T-ECC and gaining experience for 1 year is equal to working in other counterparts for 2year to gain same experience.
5. Grant leave when ever needed.
6. If any one interested in Construction Management, This is the best company to work for. As it takes challenging projects and believes in quality.
column11:
1. Hectic working hours
2. Less basic facilities available  (during working hours) for engineers working on field
3. Only Sunday is considered as weekend and gives only 7 holidays per year (excluding Sundays)
4. Administrative staffs are not much supportive to the engineers

column3: GET and 1.5 years in the company
column10:
The best part is the  on job training. from the day of ur completion of safety induction , u will be given the team and responsibility. I have team of 4 foreman reporting to me from my first day on the site(Have learnt the more about construction from them then in my BE). The Project manager allow u to do mistake and learn. Most of the GETs are of 21 to 22 years old when they join and at this stage itself they are trusted and are made section incharge. There is huge learning both technical and  management. 
The best part is its brand name and if u joined the firm as GET it increases the value further. As most of GETs after 2 or 3 years leave to further study. L&T name on resume help u to get pass MBA admission interview. 

The Initiative for GET in company is also good like opportunity to study MTech in IITs or MBA in IIMs/ XLRI
column11: Downside is work life balance. the work is at least 12-14 hours a day. The stress level are also very high. And on site it is very difficult to have people of ur age group. column10:
Yes, it is a good opportunity to work at L&T.

L&T is one company which offers challenging career opportunities. L&T is primarily an Engineering Procurement and Construction organisation which provides complete solutions to its clients.

For Mechanical Engineers, the career opportunities include in the roles of Design, Engineering, Construction, Manufacturing, Project Management etc in the fields of Power, Hydrocarbon, Engineering Services, Capital goods manufacturing, Mining, Process plants, Defence, Nuclear and various other core mechanical engineering industries.

L&T is one company which works on some of the most complex projects and product developments that are currently being undertaken in India : Nuclear Submarines, Warships, Metros, Large bridges, Factory Infrastructure, Offshore and Onshore platforms, Engineering Solutions. You can be part of any one of the teams.

You join as an Engineer / Trainee Engineer and can progress in your career to become Managers, Deputy General Managers, General Managers, Vice President, Executive Vice President and finally President.

column3: Recommend only for money and Name of Company
column10:
1)The best place for any fresher to gain experience on construction methodologies/technologies, machinery, labour management etc.
2)For a civil engineer a place to become experts on particularly Formwork as it is a well established system in L&T Construction.
3)Get acquainted to wonderful thumb rules and heuristic problem solving.
4)Almost free accommodation, free transport, and other such benefits help the work life get moving.
5)Brand Value. Opens up other vistas.
column11:
1)Very long working hours. (in my case I work 14-15 hrs a day for 6 days a week). Plus construction sites are 24x7 in many cases. Means Night Shifts/ Sundays working. (works up to at least a good 80 hours a week)
2)Payments/Promotions totally depend on the annual rating. And this rating totally depends on your PM. So one is screwed if his rating is low. Sudden changes in ratings require detailed explanation from the PM so they avoid it as much as possible.
3)Even as a trainee you'll be given very high responsibilities without giving any proper training. Mostly you'll end up at the mercy of your mentor. (still many companies dont consider the training period as an experience)
4)Nothing like a corporate environment.
5)For the time put on working the payment is very low compared to other industries, but competitive within the construction industry. My friends in IT majors like Oracle, TI etc put at least 20-25 hours lesser than me and still get paid 3 times of what I'm getting paid.

column10: Best place to learn and get experienced in the Civil Engineering. The company is far ahead of other companies in terms of technology (in India). Also the company looks out for its employees, specially if you are a GET. The salary ranges of a GET are from 38-44 K in hand, depending on the fact whether you are on site or office works. Site gets the higher pay. And of course the work on site also needs more hours to be devoted. The accommodation depends on site to site, if you are working for a high budget project, above 500 Cr, then you will probably get good accommodation, but that's but that's not always the hard and fast case. You will most likely be accommodated in a flat with other employees, wherein you will be in a double sharing room, and there will be a cook for every batch of 10-12 employees. You don't have to pay for the cook, the company takes care of that for you.
column11: The working hours are devastating at times, 8:00-8:00 on site usually. If you are from the North side of India then language is going to be a bit of an issue. L&T has a majority of employees from South India, but even though they there is are regional differences, everyone at L&T are very accommodating. Also like I mentioned earlier, accommodation could at times be very poor.

column10:
Larsen & Toubro Limited (L&T) is a technology, engineering, construction and manufacturing company. It is one of the largest and most respected companies in India's private sector.
 
More than seven decades of a strong, customer-focused approach and the continuous quest for world-class quality have enabled it to attain and sustain leadership in all its major lines of business.
 
L&T has an international presence, with a global spread of offices. A thrust on international business has seen overseas earnings grow significantly. It continues to grow its global footprint, with offices and manufacturing facilities in multiple countries. 
 
The company's businesses are supported by a wide marketing and distribution network, and have established a reputation for strong customer support.
 
L&T believes that progress must be achieved in harmony with the environment. A commitment to community welfare and environmental protection are an integral part of the corporate vision.
 
In response to changing market dynamics, L&T has gone through a phased process of redefining its organisation model that facilitates growth through greater levels of empowerment. The new structure is built around multiple businesses designated ‘Independent Companies’ or ‘ICs’.
column11:
L&T has a global presence. A thrust on international business over the years has seen overseas revenues growing steadily.
 
The company has manufacturing facilities in India, the Middle East, South East Asia, China and Australia.
 
Customers include global majors in over 30 countries.

Favourism is all they (management) know.Job culture is very poor. We do design only in names but in actual practice the job is basically a job of a Middle man. No scope of improvement. Middle and senior management are not at all very keen to learn any any thing no they want to educate the entry level engineers. column3: Good learning opportunities in different sectors of construction/manufacturing industry
column10: Candidates are given opportunity to handle additional responsibilities at lower levels, young employees are given good opportunities for growth, job security
column11: extra long working hours, inflexible transfer postings after recent creation of independent companies within L&T, widespread nepotism - especially in construction division, about 5% average annual pay increment (which is very low) in the initial 5-7 years, frequent restructuring of the company in recent years has spoiled the culture and diluted the quality of professionalism within the company, low job satisfaction if stuck in the wrong division as initial posting in the company is done on random basis as per requirement of certain divisions and not based on candidate's preference

column3: Very backward, full of papers, no technology.If you are an MBA graduate from premier institute, dont join this company
column10:
1. Lunch costs 10 rupees. 
2. Salary is credited on the 28th of every month. 
Except for these 2 factors, there are no pros
column11:
1. They still live in the '80s. Haven't updated any processes. All processes happen on paper. You will be shocked to see the number of papers they waste every single hour. 
2. Management is filled with old people who have a lot of ego issues and expect everything to be done in their own ways. They don't update themselves with changing generations. They don't know the word "work from home"
3. Worst HR policies. Sharp 9 AM in-time and then it goes till 8 or 9 night. You will be asked to come on holiday also. Formal Dress code though its HQ. It feels like school. People who stay for long hours are glorified and the person who works productively for 8 hours and leaves at 5 30 is taunted.
4. Pathetic onboarding. It takes 15 days to get an ID card, laptop, etc. For everything, you have to go behind people multiple times to get things done.
5. There are covid cases inside the campus right now and still they don't care. They dont let news come out.
6. Theres no role clarity.

column3: Worst company - never go unless you are desperate for a job
column10: I don't see any pros other than they have a lot of openings.
column11:
1. You need to literally beg to get reimbursements for some amount you spend for official purpose. Old school procedures and no one cares to even reply to your mails.
2. HR doesn't care about anything. Never expect replies unless you resign
3. Joining bonus scam is there. LTTS would say that they will buy you out and at joining time, they will come up with a policy that you have to show the payment we made to company. Most companies will deduct leaves and relieve employees and if we ask for it, HR says 'Oh, we dont have a policy for that' after we join.
4. If you are deputed to UK or US onsite, LTTS would deduct employer and employee contribution of PF from you taxable salary there. Though its illegal and even after notifying umpteen times, LTTS doesn't bother to care at all.
5. The company recently blocked access to email and teams of atleast a 100 employees worldwide as they didn't complete some mandatory ISMS training on time. This will give you a clarity on why not to choose LTTS. (Blocking email means the employee can't even raise the query evenif he had already attended the training , or if he wants to download medical insurance card)

column3: A really nice place to learn, but not a great place to earn
column10: L&T is a great place to learn, especially if its in the initial stages of your career. It being a large multinational there are loads of impressive project going on simultaneously and with the people there being very easy to approach, there is great opportunity to learn whats happening in the advancements of technology.
column11: Very long work hours and very hectic work schedule; plus no appropriate compensation for the work. The work-money ratio is not properly balanced. I have heard many people who are on the lower levels of management complaining of the work hours and pay; with many other competitor companies paying nearly double of what L&T pays for nearly half of the work load. It seems to be a good place to just spend a few starting career years there.

column3: Best Lala Company with the most regressive corporate folks of planet earth
column10:
- Everyone in this company who holds a high position is a friend of a friend of someone influential. So, if you are an incompetent bootlicker with contacts at high places, this is the best company to settle down
- The management strongly believes that MBA fellas are postmen. So, if you are an MBA...
- Babu Culture - So, good food and free tea, coffee 2-3 times a day
column11:
- Irrespective of whether you are an MBA from IIM or an engineer from IIT, you will treated be as the dumbest person on earth unless you butter your boss
- Human rights do not exist within the premises of this office (I am not even talking about project sites)
- This company neither lets you live in peace, nor lets you leave in peace (takes 3 months+ for F&F settlement and are not even transparent about how much the company owes to the employer)
- There is a King who believes Corona doesnt exist. As a result, company policy doesnt allow leave even if you get corona. WFH is an alien concept. 
- Due to 2 months of lockdown, every Saturday has been made a working day till end of FY 2020. Need I say more?

Work Life Balance
Long Working Hours

column10:
Good exposure. Great place to start a career. 
One may take initiatives towards new business development and proposal is heard and acknowledged by all top management folks.
If you are pro-active, you can get yourself involved in many things and you can take up big tasks responsibility.
column11:
Traditional workplace culture is a big disadvantage in L&T. 
If you want the senior to be fair and kind, get ready to adapt yes-sir and no-sir kinda environment around.
The top-down approach and biased decisions go hand in hand. Appraisals are more influenced by boot-legging rather than one's effectiveness and performance. This is the main reason people don't hold on to this company. the ones who do are either intrigued by their work or they are the part of that same old-fashioned work environment.

column3: The most horrible company ever!!! -L&T engineering, Faridabad.
column10: You get so desperate to get out of there that you make your best efforts at securing another job or go for higher studies. Hardly 10% of the GETs who joined with me in L&T Faridabad remained after 2 years. Why 2 years you ask? Well, that's 'cause there is a bond that you cannot leave until 2 years  or else you have to pay a fine of 200,000. Even this is a huge amount when they pay only 20,000 each month.
column11: Horrible work culture. Bosses treat their sub-ordinates like they were slaves. They even have separate restrooms,called COVENANT Restrooms for employees who are above a particular management designation (I am not kidding) and general restrooms for those who are below that grade. Low salary. Low salary growth. Lot of politics. I have seen my SBU head lie to the client. So, there goes "Work Ethics". Particularly, people in L&T Faridabad have very poor communication skills in English and they harbor feelings of animosity against anybody who has good communication skills. I have never received any appreciation for any work I have done, even when I have done a great job. It really lowered my self esteem.

column8: No work life balance, lack of coordination

column3: Terrible Work-Life balance, Poor Compensation & Hostile Management
column10: Nothing good to say about this company, I joined as an Engineer right out of college, they measure work by checking how many hours the engineer is spending at site even if there is no work, the petty politics here are absurd, boring and monotonous work, they expect you to be at site 13-15 hrs a day and doesn't pay enough compensation to make up for it. That's why the attrition rate here in fresh college hires are very high, if you are from a well respected college do not join this company, there are several trainings available for other fields - consider them!
column11:

column3: Exploitation and Politics in the name of MNC
column10:
1. GET gets promoted quickly when compared to others.

column11:
1. The number of working hours are too high.
2. JETs or lateral entry personnel are not given the same respect as GETs.
3. No 'work from home' during high COVID period.
4. No knowledge with respect to Design in Vizag.
5. High politics.
6. Biased rating system. When asked for feedback, "You are good but others are better" is what is told to the employees.
7. The actual bosses who stay in Powai never come and talk to the employees. Even if they do, they don't resolve the issues which are faced at the ground level.
8. FTCs are not given a hike at all. Contract staff and FTC staff are not given respect unless they are related to a Naval officer.
9. The top management does not care about the employees or their health. Leaves are not given at the required time. People who were just recovered from COVID were also called to office, without caring about their body pains.
10. Highly political company.
11. No work life balance at all.
12. Almost everything that is to be done is hidden the carpet with a single lie- "we work in the customer premises and we cannot do anything regarding it".

column3: only brand name rest all is empty
column10:
Good place to pick up skills/experience in short to medium term. (Max 5 years)
Sufficient responsibilities and freedom is given as far as work is concerned
column11:
1. Deplorable decision making skills especially when entire business/org is concerned (for eg whether to declare off due to excessive rains, strike, or even recent lockdown situation - We in Mumbai got WFH orders only 3 days after Mah. Govt Orders) 
2. The HR and Compensation Benefit Policies are very prehistoric in nature. There has been no pay revisions in last 10 years and annual increments are typically in range of 2% - 5% only. Only the Top 1% get decent allowances but the rest get very very less. Basic Component is deliberately kept low which affects your EPF and Eligibility of Bank Loans,Credit Cards etc.
3. Empathy and Dignity for lower level employees is less as line managers are promoted without being imparting necessary soft skills on team handling 
4. Performance and Benefits depend solely on how you are rated and what is your equation with the seniors 
5. Work Life Balance depends solely on your manager and HOD 
6. It is vast Org. so culture varies from place to place and business to business but mainly depends on the business head.

column3: hectic experience but at the end it's helpful

2. It is the best construction company in india, so you dont have to give a second thought regarding joining, but at the cost of leaving behind your social life 
3. I was in civil engg. dept, and to be honest I had a great time working there for 2 years
column11:
1. Long working hours.
2. Higher management will try to dominate over you all the time, so be tricky, you will survive

column3: Larsen and Toubro EAIC group ( Electrical and Automation)
column10:

column11:

*Nothing new to learn - Copy products from competitors and make minor changes to software

column3: Good camaraderie but slow decision making, nepotism. Not very goal oriented

Not professional in internal projects. Employee satisfaction surveys are just an eye wash. 
Team building / outings are nil. 
Outbound training is limited to management development centers.
Old technologies used. Upgradation and capital investments take years to come through.

column3: L&T Shipbuilding is not a place where you will learn or grow professionally
column10: I worked for the Shipbuilding Division in Chennai, India. There are very few pros to this particular division. I was lucky to have a good set of co-workers and colleagues (excluding bosses) including many from my own age group. Even though work was pretty dull and stagnant, the good people made up for it in a way.
column11: The bosses are all people who have retired from the Navy and were brought in for their experience. While this is an advantage in many respects, they brought their naval heirarchies into the office. It felt like working for a government office with respect to the pace of work, red tape and level of motivation. Also, the HR department is one of the worst in the country. I know this is probably said for all companies but I mean it when I say that our HR department was totally ineffective, starting with the General Manager. Very inefficient and casual with no respect for the remaining employees. All in all, not a pleasant experience professionally.

column3: A great name to work with......
column10: Respect for the name (from outsiders), Access to huge markets & great customers - thus exciting challenges at work and importantly, for some of the best colleagues/friends you would ever make in any company.
column11: Lack of trust w.r.t information sharing between seniors and subordinates, no clear role definition in most cases and in some cases, no organization charts as well  - this results in a lot of inefficiency at work !

column10: Salary will be credited on time
column11: I think words will be minimal in expressing the cons.there are hundreds of cons like Rubbish people as your manager who even haven't any basic knowledge of engineering.they will talk like they know everything but they don't know anything.they will mentally harass you. your all potential will be exhausted without any achieved.they will always try to let you feel lower.no work culture, no English speaking people.they will press you to do wrong things.whether that is violating company rule or not , doesn't matter.worst HR people,they will not respond to any mail written by you if it contains any complain regarding manager's act.no fix time , violation of labour laws,violation of minimum wages law for RS staff.even some time management will abuse you.only chamchas get a good rating.your performance doesn't matter.finally I would like to say, if you love your career,life & want to live a healthy & prosperous life then better to not to choose L&T.i have many more things to write but I can't.

column10:
Hello everyone!! Every budding civil engineer would have marked L&T as a construction company to go for. Yes it is! 
We leverage company on salary, 'being famous', employee life. Considering pay package CTC, L&T pays the best in the construction company standards, good bonus, on time salary. Fame wise everyone recognizes the company in INDIA. Growth in the company is very good compared to other construction companies. Manager cadre can be achieved in 4 yrs, if hard work pays off. Company benefits are the best, in terms of health benefits, travel reimbursement , company get together, employee training.
column11: Considering the enormous benefits the company offers, anyone will definitely give the best shot to the company. We work hard. But the company fails to evaluate everyone on the same scale. Work life balance is the worst. The companies culture to work drains the energy of the employee which makes him forget all the good things the company does.

column3: A good company to work with
column10:
1. Above average salary
2. Good workplace culture if your location is in Tier 1 cities or near Corporate office.
3. Good for people who take a planned vacation only once a year :D
column11:
1. Difficult to get your Holidays approved. Even for a 4-5 days holiday you will have to plan 3-4 months in advance.
2. The longest holiday you will get is one week that too only if you are getting married or for something similar( once in a lifetime events)
3. Your work life is highly dependent on your boss. 
4. Best case is your boss is not a lateral hire & joined L&T as an GET, otherwise your life is difficult since lateral joiners bring in some bad culture from their old corporates. 
5. Again a totally different work culture for remote locations or even Tier 2 cities. I believe the root cause again to be a lot of lateral hiring that takes place for such locations.

column10: L&T is a highly professional organisation with huge opportunity for learning and taking technical responsibilities in multiple functions. It is very strong EPC and Technology driven company operating globally. Work culture is open and transparent. Training and development is continuous and company gives good overall performance based remuneration and various perks like Car scheme, Company Housing ,Club & Holiday Home’s Facilities for senior managers .It’s best company for long term stay and to build leadership in area of your expertise. It’s very widely respected by partners , vendors , Customers and Collaborators. Learning is continuous in high technology domain.
column11: It’s long working hours and Saturdays working for factory area offices as well. Tremendous work pressure to perform against peers from cross functional roles. Pressure to Every time perform in new role and assigned ardent  tasks.

column10: Employees are allowed to take decision on major things. Growth in terms of role may be good for outstanding candidates after completing 2-3 years in company. An opportunity to work for esteemed projects of national importance. The candidate will surely learn to deal with stress meeting the deadlines.
column11:
Too hectic. No work-life balance at all. Freshers are asked to fill a bond of two years (or two lakhs) which may be paid on a pro-rata basis of your HR agrees to it. They may make trouble for the candidate on this matter. In my opinion the HR system of the company is not upto the mark. Employees face lot of trouble specially when posted on site. At some sites not even basic necessities like clean water (both for drinking and for other use) is not available, being in outskirts any means for recreation is not there etc.
Only people who are very much sure in making a career in construction industry and are very passionate about the same should opt this.

column3: Gives a steep learning curve. Best place to be in if learning is your only priority and not earning

1) Very good peers

column10:

column10: Implementing and constantly monitoring policies on employee/management relations is an exceptionally good idea. Make sure the policies are in place and working effectively. If they have the assurance that their voices and concerns will be heard, the employee feels more confident in their place in the organization.
column11: Infractions of policies and rules are bound to happen. What will make your employees feel your company is a great place to work is the knowledge that any breach of conduct will be dealt with fairly and quickly. Employees see a firmly established and well-executed plan for infractions as a protective device and quality workers will appreciate it.

column10:
1- Timely Salary every month
2-Brand Name since ages
column11:

2-Complicated pay structure with a lot of components which are deducted/paid later and not with monthly salary
3-Saturday half working day  
4-Slow career growth, leading to layoff
5-Poor pay master

7-You may end up doing a clerks' job apart from the profile mentioned in the JD

column3: “Customer Service Representative/Supervisor, specialty in installation and technical support.”

column11:

column3: Pros and Cons of L&T Construction (B&F IC)
column10:
+ The best place for any fresher to gain experience on construction methodologies/technologies, machinery, labour management etc.

+ For a civil engineer a place to become experts on particularly Formwork as it is a well established system in L&T Construction.

+ Get acquainted to wonderful thumb rules and heuristic problem solving.

+ Almost free accommodation, free transport, and other such benefits help the work life get moving.

+ Brand Value. Opens up other vistas.
column11:
- Very highly imbalanced work timings. This means almost NIL life after work.

- Very long working hours. (in my case I work 14-15 hrs a day for 6 days a week). Plus construction sites are 24x7 in many cases. Means Night Shifts/ Sundays working. (works up to at least a good 80 hours a week)

- No gap between projects/ no breaks. When one project nears completion you can immediately expect transfers to another peak project. So there's no rest throughout.

- Payments/Promotions totally depend on the annual rating. And this rating totally depends on your PM. So one is screwed if his rating is low. Sudden changes in ratings require detailed explanation from the PM so they avoid it as much as possible.

- Very difficult to get leaves approved. And since its 6 days working compulsorily and 7 days working more or less every week, time for even doing chores like shopping etc wont be possible.

- For the time put on working the payment is very low compared to other industries, but competitive within the construction industry. My friends in IT majors like Oracle, TI etc put at least 20-25 hours lesser than me and still get paid 3 times of what I'm getting paid.

- Absolutely ZERO design/engineering. L&T is a contractor and designer, primarily (although L&T does have design&build projects). Therefore its only are of concern is material procurement, arrangement, logistics, labour management and implementation. Lets just say more than half the people wont even know the concepts about Bending Moment, Shear Force, The "Why" and "How" etc.

- Even as a trainee you'll be given very high responsibilities without giving any proper training. Mostly you'll end up at the mercy of your mentor. (still many companies dont consider the training period as an experience)

- Nothing like a corporate environment.

column10:
-Accomodation, cook etc. all provided at a nominal cost of Rs 100/-
-Good learning. Regular training programs at all levels.
-Great brand value in market. People will be awed to learn you work in L&T. Job shifting will also be easy.
-Timing at cluster and HQ are very relaxed. Not that strict.
-Although, regular salary less than market average. Performance Linked Reward bonuses are good. 1-2 Lakh per annum based on your rating. But can be as low as 20k p.a. for below average rating.
-You can climb up to the level of Chairman if you have talent, but only technical talent won't matter much here as you need to have good interpersonal skills more than technical skills
-Most importantly, one of the only few companies which is still recruiting civil engineering freshers.
column11:
-Timing at sites are inhumane. Minimum 12 hours, can extend upto 15 hours. No saturday or sunday. Even for going home on Holi and Diwali, you need to take permission.
-Regular salary is low and if you are not on good terms with your boss, your performance bonus can also be low. 
-Politics is ubiquitous here. You need to be very careful about what you do and what you say.
-Everyone cheats everyone, and no-one trusts anyone.
-Promotions are slow. That too based on rating.
-When everything from salary to promotions depend upon your rating, you would expect a rigorous, fool-proof and reliable rating system having measurable parameters affecting it. Instead, rating is just random. You are on the mercy of your superior.
-Old school management. Should improve the quality of system, so that there is more control without any leak.

column3: A good company to work with which has extensive reach in the construction field , however it has its own pro's & cons.
column10:
The few pros of working in L&T shall be 
i) Timely payment - Every month the payment will be credited on 28th without any delay irrelevant to the market situation.
ii) Facilities provided for staff for example bachelor accommodation for site team, family accommodation , health benefits for covenanted cadre and above , site allowance etc. 
iii) Apart , from the above mentioned there are other things like work culture, a stipulated process or system has been laid for the staffs to follow , innovating or venturing into the digitalization even at remote sites. 
iv) Separate in campus online courses are available for staffs to increase or improve themselves.
v) Easy to approach or reach the HR or management team for any help required. 
These are some of the pros.
column11:
These are the reasons why i gave only 3 star rating , 

i) Work life balance shall be totally zero while your working in site and may be even at office sometime. 
ii) Using your own PL or CL shall require approval from your immediate supervisor and sometimes it can be denied too. 
iii) Place of deputation shall be totally opposite to your living or desired location, i.e. a person from will be deputed north to south and vice versa . There is no problem in deputing them on temporary basis like for a year or two or max three years but the management will not change this pattern nor approve any transfer request from the staff until they wish for it to happen .
iv) Appraisal , Rating & Promotion duration can be disappointing majorly because every other project manager wont be cool enough to give maximum rating or even a good rating for that matter for the works that you do. Many rating given by most of PM's in the company are Biased only few PM's are there who give or rate for the work completion and involvement shown by the staff.
v) And the usual office politics and biased reviews even prevail here like other big MNC's.

column3: Diverse, Rewarding but a lot left to Chance
column10:
1. Diverse products.
2. There is a lot to learn outside your job function or role. Almost everything is transparent in processes except costs and one can learn about how a world-class organization function. The products might not be the best-in-class, some of the systems might be a pain because of how people run them but the systems were designed to be excellent.
3. Invests a lot in training. We have a training centers and I loved the company's investment in this aspect.
4. Heavy Engineering Division and Civil Engineering (ECC) provide an extremely rewarding experience in their respective fields but as mentioned in the title a lot is left to chance. You might get a department like marketing even though you wanted manufacturing and the experience varies a lot depending on the product-line. Some product lines are built in-house and it is a lot of fun being in the divisions dealing with these while some are outsourced making the experience not so exciting if you do not want to deal with vendors frequently.
5. Words cannot describe all the options available, extremely large company with nine operating companies under the parent company. Each operating company is extremely and the culture within each company and division is different. Its like having nine different companies with different products, services and cultures.
6. If you are persistent about getting a transfer to another company within the organization or another department, you should get one depending on your performance after a couple of years. Its just that when you join fresh from college, you might not have a chance to pick a preference
column11:

column3: B&F IC: If you are not a civil engineer, avoid at all costs. Would have rated it with 0 stars if allowed.
column10:
Well, you get a company accommodation near the site you have been posted to. 
And depending on the  city you are in, it can either be great or absolutely crappy.
The company has a no fire policy, but they don't really need to do that as they will pressurize you enough into leaving it anyway.
column11:
Where do I start.
Well, as the company is touted as a pinnacle of Engineering, believe me, once you are posted at site, you would need the brain of a mouse to do the work you are assigned. If you are not a civil engineer, be prepared to learn a lot about concrete because that is what the entire company revolves around. Your pleas and requests to be shifted into your own branch will fall on deaf ears. Once you have been assigned to a site, you are its resource, to be used as the management pleases. 
That brings me to the quality of the management. The top management at L&T is brilliant. The people are smart and effective, and utterly clueless as to how to solve the problems faced at site. All of them will quote that they worked at site initially, but nobody will say how they never were really posted at site dealing with execution. The management at site are ineffective, lazy and tyrannical at best. Most of them know nothing beyond their lives within the company and have no desire to do so either. As an educated individual from one of the best colleges in the country, it becomes a hassle to deal with seniors who have been so thoroughly washed with the L&T culture, that every different idea you bring to the table will be frowned upon.
Well this is turning into an endless rant.
Summing up
Joint the B&F IC as a GET/GAT for the following reasons :
- You love being bonded over a period of time. (2 years or 2 lacs, upto you)
- You hate having a social life and following your dreams.
- You want a job that will pay you for standing in the sun and supervise labor day through night.
-  You hate the concept of creating and want to be in a rut for the rest of your life.
If not, and you really want to cultivate a career that will make you successful proud of yourself as an individual, then stay the f*ck away from this company. Heck, its like a BPO without the computers and the ACs.

column11:
Oh.. where do I start.
1. Long working hours (I mean 14-16 hour long).
2. Authoritative senior management. Its like they payed you so they will exploit you and unashamedly. 
3. No Sundays or holidays. (No pay for the extra work.)There was a point of time when I actually spent 9 continuous Sundays working with no holidays i.e about two months of continuous work. Yet got screwed by my manager for not turning up the following Sunday. Also we worked on republic day.
4. They said lot of training programs for GETs. yet in my whole year I got only 4 days training.
5. As a site engineer be ready for these 3 things: Standing in the sun for long hours, Shouting at subordinates, getting shouted at by seniors (They literally bark at you).
6. If you join as a GET in all probability you will be reporting to any old time diploma engineer who has spent 20 years at the company and hardly progressed. So do not expect any special treatment.
7. Nightlife in L&T is a cold shivering winter night standing in a desolate site shouting at sleepy laborers.
8. If you think you love engineering and want to join L&T then don't be fooled because "THERE IS NO TRUE ENGINEERING INVOLVED IN ANY SITE" its only managing laborers and resources.

Finally a word of advice ; If you are a self respecting engineer who is passionate about your subject and think you can succeed no matter what then join any thing but a construction company. It will literally suck the life out of you.

column10:
- Job Security 
- Five o Clock Snacks (if you are in Chennai L&T Office) 
- Well Organised Recreation Club (again if you are in Chennai L&T Office)
- Good Place for the people who had either retired or taken VRS from various Govt. organizations
- Good place for the people who give absolute importance to the family 
- Good brand name for GET's to carry in the CV for their job interviews
- Good company when compared to the other worst construction companies in India
- If you do not want to work,  L&T is the best place to do it. L&T provides lot of space to search/ apply for better jobs using their internet and more importantly it will provide lot of options to do except job
-  If you are single and have 2 to 3 years before marriage, you can join L&T as you will find eligible partners with good qualifications and more importantly more free time to do some romance in the corporate premises
- If you want to do something really great, you will be allowed. But be prepared to come to terms when you will be discarded without any valid reasons and all your hard works will become irrelevant. Absolute beauty is when you will realize that somebody is getting a good name by presenting your work to the management as if he/she has done and without even a slightest reference to your hard work. (Poaching of your ideas is quite prevalent. Include this in the cons section as well).
column11:
- Without generalizing, HR system in L&T is like Indian Democracy, wherein their policies looks so interesting on papers but when it comes to reality nobody has any clue whatsoever of what their system is all about and they behave in an autocratic way to make sure nobody points fingers at their nose
-  L&T does not respect the lateral entrants who are coming with experience and they make sure that you get harassed in the initial years in order to bring down their professional ego. 
- To be successful in L&T you need to Join as a GET and retire in the same company, 
- there are so many  managers (deputy managers, assistant managers etc ). Seriously we don't know if such people have real capabilities for holding the managerial positions or can render decisions from such positions. Too many people are involved in the same job which means too much of interference affecting the quality of the work
- Highest level of bureaucracy in the management
- Management/ Managers never stand according to their commitments / promises. You will find their promises keeps changing like passing clouds until such time you will get fed up and leave the company. 
- Talents are not recognized unless and until if you know the tactics to keep pacifying your boss (applicable for both the genders and their individual pacifying capabilities). Few exceptions do exist and there are some good leaders in the company; but unfortunately they are only few in numbers and not all end up with such charismatic leaders 
- You will find some exceptionally talented people whom are either being under utilized or unrecognized mainly because of the poor management
- Salary is not in line with the market standards. They will lure you with the CTC but you will end up realizing that almost 50% of your salary is apportioned in to employee benefit sections (variable pay, performance linked pay, TLR, phone allowance, Tyre allowance, PF, blah blah... Man please give me my money. I am wise enough to decide upon how to spend it. :) :) :)  And most importantly none of these benefits are released in one go. They are carefully designed to be released in different phases of the year in order to create the mental barrier for the employee to keep reconsidering his decision to leave the company. Nevertheless, you will loose for sure some portion of your pay benefits when you leave the company. 
- Highest level of negativity among the lateral entrant employees for their dissatisfaction towards the management and personnel. L&T's attrition rate is mainly contributed by the exit of many lateral entrants.

column10: If you don't want to learn anything new. Not comfortable with computers and still like to work with pen and paper. This is the organization for you.
column11:
No flexi timing - you have to report at sharp 9:00 AM. If you punch in at 9:01AM - your half day salary will be deducted.

Power distance - Your manager will treat you like slave. There is no first name basis. No open door policy. Very strict hierarchy. If you are running late for office even by 30 minutes and will reach by 9:30. You will have to inform your manager. If you are late and you know that your half day salary will be deducted, even then you have to come to office and work for free.

No work from home:
 - Even during pandemic time almost all the employees were called to office and no WFH was given. This caused many covid cases within the office premises, even then the company called the employees to office. There are no special covid leave given to employees. Many people died because of such policies of the organization. Even during such a large second wave of covid, company has taken special approval and asking employees to come to office (Even for the non-essential people who can easily WFH).  Because of this many people within the organization have died and the company is suppressing such news to come out.
- During the Flood situation, all the staffs were asked to report to office. Even though you die while coming to office they don't care. You will have to report by 9:00AM. If you don't report at 9:00 AM your salary will be deducted and your manager will scold you separately.

Employee Hygiene - There are two types of toilets in L&T. One is for Leadership People and other is for rest of the employee (including office boys and other support staff). They will not provide with even paper towels in toilets. Everyone need to use one common Towel  (Cotton) in the toilet. Moreover, the toilets are so small and less in number that you will always have to wait for your turn (You may feel you come in a dungeon to pee with gloomy and dim lights - like an infrastructure of 50 years old). And this is the state of the Head Office of L&T in Mumbai. You can imagine the state of rest of the country.

Work - The work that these people do is of some different century. They are still working on pen and paper and the old chaps sitting in the top positions won't allow new systems and procedures to come in. The world has moved on to a different century and these people are in a different era all together. They feel that by giving the salary on 28th of every month is a favor to employee. Other then salary their are no other benefits that they give.

Staggered Salary - They are very smart in giving the salary. They will camouflage your CTC as 17.30LPA but in hand salary will be 75K per month. You will start earning less then your previous company. Most of the salary component will be bifurcated into retention bonus (payable once every year), Housing Rent (22K PM - will be given on claim basis) and PLVA (once per year that too in the month of June). Hence, whenever you will resign - You will have to let go a major part of your salary.

True L&Tite: They sell such toxic work culture by saying to become a true L&Tite. Where you need to serve the country and die yourself. But the truth is - people in Leadership level are roaming in company driven Mercedez in a very different office space, while the rest of the employees are underpaid and made to work in such poor working condition that no one can imagine.

I learnt so much from the first job I ever had. Not the things that would help me advance in my career directly, but it helped in clearing my mind up. It made me strong and aware of the realities of various kinds of people we could come across. 
However, since this question asks something different, I shall answer it by saying NO,it is not a stimulating experience.
column11:
the job is not worth it. I worked for 12 months as a GET in three different sites. The first site was where I had good seniors and managers. I did learn a few basic things but nothing that would enable me to advance in my career. It was a job which could be performed by anyone.

When I was transferred to my second site, I realized how thankless and demeaning the job that I performed with full capabilities actually was. There were days when I was shouted upon for doing the 'right' thing. There were days when I would end up with torn and dirty trousers. I also suffered for a week from welder's flash. 

I couldn't take it anymore and I thought that taking a transfer to a better site would change things. However, my experience at the third site also wasn't any better. One day, i was asked to work with a team overnight and when the Project Manager left, the team vanished and there was nobody left. I ended up waiting for an hour and  then arranging my own transport to travel home ( which was 30 kms from the site). It was raining heavily and I reached home with a tired and dejected mind. I also reached a point where I fell from a small height(ENTIRELY MY FAULT) and ended up twisting my ankle. I had suffered from ligament tears earlier in college and after this incident, there was a noticeable swelling in my ankle again. I had to take a sick leave for three weeks and upon my return, I was shouted at and asked, " Why do they hire engineers like you? What disease do you have ? "

column11:
Everything is a con here, or in other words everyone here is a con.
1) Technical Work: Believe me an uneducated donkey can do your work.  12th passed, foremans,diplomo engineers and graduate engineers all do the same work without any difference. There is not something called learning curve, and as a result you will find difficult to switch jobs as others, as you have gained nothing in the years you work in L&T.

2) Team Work :  You cant find a cheaper behavior than the mob that is L&T colleagues. They will backstab you the second they get a chance, and it is never about doing the work together to achieve and rather how to do minimum work and get all the credits, how to blame other for failures, how to thoroughly clean boss's shoes to advance position with team.

3- Boss- Slave culture : there are this group called bosses/managers/immediate superiors who will write your performance evaluation which decides your bonus( equivalent to 4-5 months salary) and promotions. These people always want sir everytime you talk to them as if "Sir" title is awarded to them by the queen. For Example " Ok sir, I will do this. Surely " will irk the  boss - they expect " Ok sir, I will do this sir. Surely sir" , and also stupid things like not sitting in front of them, buying grocery for them.

4: Boot-licking and performance- Out of 10 guys who gets quick promotions , 8 are surely boot-lickers. As the boss doesn't have to justify his ratings for bonus and promotions it is more important to please him whichever way it is convenient. In all my years , I have seen many Construction managers who cant read a single drawing properly who are at this position only because they are a pet of some Vice President.
